Yolulo · Autozod
Settings

Autozod Settings & Account

Manage your Autozod profile, password, two-factor authentication, browser sessions, API tokens, and team membership.

System Autozod
Role Admin
Updated March 2026
← Hub ↑ Autozod Profile Password & 2FA Sessions Delete Account API Tokens Team
Settings → Profile

Profile Information

Update the Autozod account name and email address used for your organisation's Autozod login.

💡
Navigation: Autozod top nav → Settings — or — click the account name (top right) → Profile
Profile page — update Name and Email, then click SAVE.
Profile Information Fields
Name
Display name for the Autozod account (e.g. Yolulo). Shown in the top-right account menu.
Email
The email address used to log in to Autozod. Changing this changes the login email.
SAVE
Click to save name and email changes.
Settings → Security

Update Password & Two-Factor Authentication

Change the account password and optionally enable 2FA for additional login security.

Update Password section (Current, New, Confirm) and Two Factor Authentication toggle.
Update Password
Current Password
Enter the existing password to verify identity before changing it.
New Password
Enter the new password. Use a long, random string for security.
Confirm Password
Re-enter the new password — must match exactly.
SAVE
Click to apply the new password. The change takes effect immediately on next login.
Two Factor Authentication (2FA)
Status
Shown as "You have not enabled two factor authentication." when disabled.
ENABLE
Click to begin 2FA setup. When enabled, a secure random token from the Google Authenticator app is required at each login.
How it works
Install Google Authenticator on your phone, scan the QR code shown during setup, then enter the 6-digit code at each login.
🔐
Recommended: Enable 2FA on the Autozod account to protect against unauthorised access, especially as Autozod controls live driver dispatch and payout settings.
Settings → Sessions

Browser Sessions

View all active sessions and remotely log out other devices if the account may be compromised.

Browser Sessions — lists all devices currently or recently logged into the account, with device type, browser, IP address, and last activity.
Sessions List
This device
The current session is labelled This device in green.
Other sessions
Shows device type (desktop/mobile), browser, IP address, and when last active (e.g. "Last active 1 week ago").
LOGOUT OTHER BROWSER SESSIONS
Button at the bottom — terminates all sessions except the current one.
Logout confirmation — enter your password to confirm, then click LOGOUT OTHER BROWSER SESSIONS.
Logging Out Other Sessions
Password required
Enter your current password in the confirmation modal to authorise the logout.
LOGOUT OTHER BROWSER SESSIONS
Confirms the action. All other active sessions are terminated immediately.
NEVERMIND
Cancels the action — no sessions are affected.
⚠️
If the account may be compromised — log out all other sessions AND update the password immediately. The sessions list may not be exhaustive.
Settings → Danger Zone

Delete Account

Permanently delete the Autozod account and all associated data.

Delete Account — the DELETE ACCOUNT button (red) permanently removes all account data. Download any data first.
🚨
This action is irreversible. All resources and data — agents, task history, payout records, webhooks, API tokens, and team settings — will be permanently deleted. Download any required data before proceeding. Do not use this unless closing the Autozod account entirely.
Account → API Tokens

API Tokens

Create API tokens to allow third-party services (e.g. the Hyperzod platform) to authenticate with Autozod on your behalf.

💡
Navigation: Click the account name (e.g. Yolulo) in the top-right → API Tokens
Account menu (top-right "Yolulo" dropdown) — Profile, API Tokens, Logout. Team ID shown top-right of the API Tokens page.
API Tokens page — Create API Token form (top) and Manage API Tokens list (bottom). Team ID displayed top-right.
Create API Token
Name Required
A descriptive label for the token (e.g. Karooma, Hyperzod Integration). Used to identify the token in the list.
Permissions
Choose which operations this token can perform: create · read · update · delete. Tick only the permissions the integration actually needs.
CREATE
Click to generate the token. The token value is shown once — copy it immediately and store it securely.
Manage API Tokens
Token list
Shows all existing tokens with their name and last used timestamp (e.g. Last used 36 minutes ago).
Permissions link
Click Permissions to view or edit the permission set for an existing token.
Delete link
Click Delete to permanently revoke the token. Any integration using it will immediately lose access.
Team ID
Shown top-right of the page. Required when configuring integrations that need to identify the Autozod team (e.g. the Hyperzod webhook setup).
API Token Permissions modal — edit which operations (create, read, update, delete) are granted to an existing token. Click SAVE to apply changes.
Account → Team Management

Team Settings & Members

Manage the Autozod team — rename it, add admin or editor members, view pending invitations, and create or switch between teams.

💡
Navigation: Click the team name (e.g. Yolulo Drivers) in the top-right → Team Settings or Create New Team
Team dropdown (top-right "Yolulo Drivers") — Manage Team, Team Settings, Create New Team, Switch Teams.
Team Settings — rename the team and add new team members by email.
Team Name
Team Owner
Read-only — shows the account name and email of the team owner.
Team Name
Editable name for the team (e.g. Yolulo Drivers). Shown in the top-right team switcher.
SAVE
Click to save the updated team name.
Add Team Member — enter email and select role (Administrator or Editor). Pending Invitations shows invited users awaiting acceptance.
Add Team Member
Email Required
Email address of the person to invite. They will receive an invitation email with a link to join.
Role — Administrator
Full access — can perform any action in Autozod including managing agents, payouts, and settings.
Role — Editor
Limited access — can read, create, and update but cannot delete or manage team settings.
ADD
Sends the invitation email. The invitee appears in Pending Team Invitations until they accept.
Pending Team Invitations
Pending list
Shows email addresses of people who have been invited but not yet accepted.
Cancel
Click the red Cancel link next to an invitation to revoke it before it is accepted.
Create New Team — enter a Team Name and click CREATE. The new team appears in the team switcher dropdown.
Create a New Team
How to access
Team dropdown (top-right) → Create New Team.
Team Owner
Pre-filled with the current account — cannot be changed.
Team Name Required
Name for the new team. Use a descriptive name (e.g. a different city or region).
CREATE
Creates the team and switches to it. All agents, tasks, and settings are per-team.
Yolulo

Autozod Settings & Account SOP · Autozod · Internal Use Only
